intake.event_framing: Assess national response choices as a novel respiratory virus spreads rapidly and hospitals begin to strain.
intake.current_development: Community transmission accelerates, hospital admissions rise, and officials debate school closures and travel limits.
known_constraints: Business shutdowns carry large economic costs
known_unknowns: How quickly households will comply with distancing guidance

summary: Officials will accept near-term economic pain to avoid hospital overload

documents.first-wave-hospitals.md: Hospital admissions rise quickly, intensive care capacity tightens, and testing shortages obscure the full spread of the virus.
documents.first-wave-restrictions.md: Officials debate school closures, travel limits, and distancing rules as community transmission accelerates.

intake.event_framing: Assess response choices during a renewed winter wave when vaccines exist but uptake and logistics lag.
intake.current_development: A winter wave lifts hospital pressure while vaccine supply exists, booster uptake lags, and officials debate targeted mandates.
known_constraints: Public fatigue reduces compliance with blanket restrictions
Cold-chain logistics and staffing limit rapid rollout
known_unknowns: Whether booster uptake can rise before hospital strain peaks

summary: Officials prefer targeted public-health measures over another broad lockdown

documents.vaccine-rollout.md: Vaccine supply is available but booster uptake lags, logistics are uneven, and health agencies push acceleration plans.
documents.winter-wave.md: A winter wave drives renewed hospital strain while public fatigue weakens compliance with broad restrictions.

case_title: Early U.S. Omicron wave and booster push
historical_outcome: U.S. health authorities emphasized booster uptake and layered prevention measures during the early Omicron wave rather than returning immediately to nationwide lockdowns.

sources.title: CDC Expands COVID-19 Booster Recommendations
sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

sources.title: SARS-CoV-2 B.1.1.529 (Omicron) Variant — United States, December 1–8, 2021

intake.event_framing: Assess U.S. response choices during the early Omicron wave in late 2021.
intake.current_development: Officials faced early Omicron spread, urged boosters, and recommended layered prevention measures to slow transmission and protect hospitals.
known_constraints: Public fatigue weakens appetite for broad shutdowns
Transmission risk is rising before the full severity picture is clear
known_unknowns: How quickly booster uptake and layered prevention can offset hospital strain

summary: Officials will emphasize boosters and layered mitigation rather than immediate nationwide lockdowns

documents.cdc-booster.md: CDC expanded booster recommendations in late November 2021 and tied them to concerns about the emerging Omicron variant.
documents.omicron-mmwr.md: CDC said concurrent prevention strategies, including vaccination, masking, ventilation, testing, quarantine, and isolation, were recommended to slow Omicron transmission.

case_title: Mpox emergency and targeted public health response in 2022
historical_outcome: Authorities escalated surveillance and targeted public-health measures during the 2022 mpox emergency rather than returning to blanket society-wide restrictions.

sources.title: 08/05/2022: Lab Advisory: HHS Declares Mpox a Public Health Emergency
sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

intake.event_framing: Assess response choices after the 2022 mpox outbreak is escalated to an international and U.S. public health emergency.
intake.current_development: The WHO declares mpox a PHEIC, HHS declares a public health emergency, and authorities expand surveillance and targeted response measures.
known_constraints: Authorities need to act quickly without reverting to blanket population-wide shutdowns
Public communication and targeted response capacity matter more than mass restrictions
known_unknowns: Whether targeted public-health action can contain spread without broader disruption

summary: Officials will favor targeted guidance and emergency public-health coordination over a lockdown-style response

documents.who-mpox.md: WHO said the multi-country mpox outbreak constituted a Public Health Emergency of International Concern in July 2022.
documents.hhs-mpox.md: CDC said HHS declared a public health emergency in August 2022 to help public health officials respond to the mpox outbreak.

historical_outcome: The U.S. response to H1N1 moved into phased vaccine rollout and priority-group targeting once vaccine became available.

sources.title: CDC H1N1 Flu | Key Facts About 2009 H1N1 Flu Vaccine
sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

sources.title: U.S. Response to 2009 H1N1 Influenza
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

intake.event_framing: Assess response choices once vaccine became available during the 2009 H1N1 pandemic response.
intake.current_development: Vaccine becomes available during the 2009 H1N1 response and authorities shift toward priority-group vaccination while transmission continues.
known_constraints: Initial vaccine supply has to be prioritized across high-risk groups
Seasonal influenza and pandemic response are overlapping
known_unknowns: How quickly vaccine rollout can reduce pressure on the health system

summary: Officials will pivot toward vaccine acceleration and targeted prioritization once supply becomes available

documents.cdc-h1n1-vaccine.md: CDC said vaccine supply was being used with priority groups and described the 2009 H1N1 vaccine rollout as a central part of the response.
documents.hhs-h1n1-response.md: HHS described the federal response to 2009 H1N1 as moving into large-scale vaccine production and deployment.
